alignments.rationale: Both versions claim asymptotic achievement of AWGN capacity with similar framing; the publication emphasizes rigorous analysis but does not change the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A power allocation scheme is introduced to improve the empirical performance of the decoder.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A power allocation scheme is introduced to improve the empirical performance of the decoder.
alignments.rationale: The power allocation contribution is present and described equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The decoding complexity can be significantly reduced by using Hadamard design matrices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The decoding complexity can be significantly reduced by using Hadamard design matrices.
alignments.rationale: The claim about complexity reduction via Hadamard matrices is unchanged between the preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Joint optimization of topology and network parameters in a smart grid can reduce power losses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The joint optimization of network topology and distributed generator parameters is essential for reducing power losses in a smart grid.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the joint-optimization claim but frames it as essential and specifies distributed generator parameters; the certainty is presented with lower confidence in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some admissible network configurations violate constraints on current and voltage at branches and nodes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some admissible network configurations violate constraints on current and voltage at branches and nodes.
alignments.rationale: The statement about constraint violations is carried over verbatim, though the publication expresses it with slightly lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Removing configurations that violate electrical constraints leads to improvements in power loss reduction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Removing configurations that violate electrical constraints from the solution space leads to important improvements in terms of power losses reduction.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same result claim about removing violating configurations improving power-loss outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The violations of electrical constraints depend solely on the topological properties of the configurations.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this 'solely topological' dependence claim, indicating it was removed or not supported in the final version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach rectifies the projection shift between the auxiliary and target domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach rectifies the projection shift between the auxiliary and target domains.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach exploits the complementarity of multiple semantic representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method exploits the complementarity of multiple semantic representations.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about exploiting complementary semantic representations is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method significantly outperforms existing methods for both zero-shot and N-shot recognition on three image and video benchmark datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach significantly outperforms existing methods for both zero-shot and N-shot recognition on three image and video benchmark datasets.
alignments.rationale: The empirical superiority result across the same tasks and datasets is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enables novel cross-view annotation tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach enables novel cross-view annotation tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the conclusion about enabling cross-view annotation tasks unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel machine learning algorithm demonstrates a root mean square error (RMSE) of less than 0.02 $m^3/m^3$ for 96% of the pixels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The root mean square error (rmse) for 96% of the pixels was less than 0.02 m3/m3.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numerical RMSE result for 96% of pixels with essentially identical wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm reduces the RMSE by up to 40% during periods of high heterogeneity in land-cover and meteorological conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific 'up to 40%' reduction or the conditional statement about high heterogeneity, so this quantitative claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Kullback Leibler divergence (KLD) between the true soil moisture and the disaggregated estimates is close to 0 for both vegetated and bare soil land covers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Kullback–Leibler divergence (KLD) between the true soil moisture and the disaggregated estimates is close to zero for both vegetated and bare-soil land covers.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same KLD result for both land-cover types with equivalent phrasing and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RMSE for the disaggregated estimates generated by the SRRM is lower than that of the Principle of Relevant Information (PRI) method on each day of the season.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rmse for the disaggregated estimates generated by the SRRM is lower than that for the principle of relevant information (PRI) method on each day of the season.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same comparative RMSE claim vs. PRI for each day of the season with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational time needed for the SRRM is reduced by three times compared to the PRI method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational time needed for the SRRM was reduced by three times compared to the PRI method.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same threefold reduction in computational time for SRRM vs PRI with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed divide-and-conquer approach on feature spaces reduces error rates compared to state-of-the-art fast SVM solvers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed divide-and-conquer approach on feature spaces reduces error rates compared to state-of-the-art fast support vector machine solvers.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same comparative claim against fast SVM solvers with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The error rates decreased by 10.53% on the RCV1 dataset and 7.53% on the covtype dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The error rates decreased by 10.53% on the RCV1 data set and 7.53% on the covtype data set.
alignments.rationale: The numeric reductions on both datasets are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel approach addresses issues of overfitting that are common in typical classification methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach addresses issues of overfitting that are common in typical classification methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the same conclusion about addressing overfitting with similar certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A scalable subspace-pursuit approach transforms the low rank plus sparse matrix decomposition problem into a subspace learning problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A scalable subspace-pursuit approach transforms the low-rank plus sparse matrix decomposition problem into a subspace learning problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological contribution with equivalent formulation and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sufficient number of sampled columns/rows for uniform random sampling is roughly O(rμ), where μ is the coherency parameter and r is the rank of the low rank component.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sufficient number of sampled columns/rows required for decomposition is roughly $\text{O}(r \mu)$, where $\mu$ is the coherency parameter and $r$ is the rank of the low-rank component.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same sampling complexity bound with equivalent symbols and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adaptive sampling algorithms make the required number of sampled columns/rows invariant to the distribution of the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adaptive sampling algorithms make the required number of sampled columns/rows invariant to the distribution of the data.
alignments.rationale: The textual claim is preserved, but the publication lowers the expressed certainty (preprint: high; publication: medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach is suitable for online implementation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach is suitable for online implementation.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert suitability for online implementation with similar wording and medium certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed spectral algorithms are based on the non-backtracking and the Bethe Hessian operators.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state the algorithms are based on the non-backtracking and Bethe Hessian operators; instead it focuses on their optimality, so the specific operator-based framing was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These algorithms are asymptotically optimal for the partial recovery problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed spectral algorithms are asymptotically optimal for the partial recovery problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same optimality claim for partial recovery with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithms detect the hidden assignment as soon as it is information theoretically possible to do so.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithms detect the hidden assignment as soon as it is information theoretically possible.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same threshold-detection claim using equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An advanced mean field theory based on the Bethe approximation is developed to describe the network state statistics of the restricted Boltzmann machine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An advanced mean-field theory based on the Bethe approximation is developed to describe the network state statistics of the restricted Boltzmann machine.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution about an advanced Bethe-based mean-field theory with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed theory provides an efficient message passing based method that evaluates the partition function and its gradients without requiring statistical sampling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides an efficient message-passing-based approach to evaluate the partition function and its gradients without requiring statistical sampling.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about an efficient message-passing approach that obviates sampling, with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results obtained from the advanced mean field theory are compared with those from the computationally expensive sampling based method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results obtained from the proposed method are compared with those from a computationally expensive sampling-based method.
alignments.rationale: Both documents describe the same comparison between the proposed analytical method and sampling-based methods with similar wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fusion of the multi-agent paradigm with evolutionary computation yields promising results in optimization problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fusion of the multi-agent paradigm with evolutionary computation yields promising results in many optimization problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds "many" which specifies the extent of applicability; the core claim (fusion yields promising results) is preserved but made more explicit/broader.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new algorithm for agent-based evolutionary computations represents individuals as fully autonomous and asynchronous agents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new algorithm for agent-based evolutionary computations allows individuals to be represented as fully autonomous and asynchronous agents.
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly (allows vs represents) but the contribution claim is the same in meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The asynchronous approach implemented in the new algorithm is both faster and more efficient in solving common optimization problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The asynchronous approach is both faster and more efficient in solving common optimization problems.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated equivalently in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Evolutionary multi-agent systems (EMAS) are more similar to biological evolution than classical evolutionary algorithms.
alignments.rationale: This claim about EMAS similarity to biological evolution does not appear in the preprint and is newly present in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Info-Greedy Sensing performance is characterized under conditions of model mismatch between the true signal and the assumed model.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance bounds are established in terms of the gap of signal posterior entropy when a mismatched covariance matrix is used.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An additional amount of power is required to achieve the same signal recovery precision when using a mismatched covariance matrix.
alignments.rationale: Same quantitative result is presented in the publication with comparable certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study explores initialization choices for Info-Greedy Sensing using either the sample covariance matrix or an efficient covariance sketching scheme.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm obtains comparable motifs in considerably less time using minimal memory.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The solution is robust to different implementation choices and offers an unprecedented degree of flexibility.

alignments.rationale: This specific constructive result (about single-component ups/downs) does not appear in the preprint claims and is introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a scheme for inferring causal relations from uncontrolled statistical data using computational algebraic geometry. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed scheme infers causal relations from uncontrolled statistical data using computational algebraic geometry tools. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same overall contribution with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide an inductive scheme for classifying functional causal structures into distinct observational equivalence classes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scheme classifies functional causal structures into distinct observational equivalence classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same classification claim; the publication maintains the same meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a procedure for deriving constraints on the joint distribution that are necessary and sufficient conditions for it to arise from a model in a given observational equivalence class. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Necessary and sufficient conditions for a joint distribution to arise from a model in a specific observational equivalence class are derived. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same derived necessary and sufficient conditions with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach allows for the determination of which causal parameters are identifiable and how to solve for these. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach identifies which causal parameters are identifiable and provides methods to solve for them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same identifiability and solution-providing claim; wording adjusted but meaning preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prospects for expanding the scope of our scheme to quantum causal inference are discussed. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prospects for expanding the scheme to quantum causal inference are discussed. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions note prospects for quantum extension with the same level of uncertainty; the categorization shifts slightly but content and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new hypothesis-free score-based causal discovery algorithm, called stable specification search, demonstrates robustness for finite samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new hypothesis-free score-based causal discovery algorithm, called stable specification search, demonstrates robustness for finite samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in publication; no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results on the simulated data set show significant improvement over alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results on the simulated data set show significant improvement over alternative approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same reported improvement on simulations; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results on the real-world data sets for Chronic Fatigue Syndrome and Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder show consistency with the hypothesis-driven models constructed by medical experts. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results on the real-world data sets for chronic fatigue syndrome and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder show consistency with the hypothesis-driven models constructed by medical experts. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same empirical consistency claim with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed smart pacing approach can effectively improve campaign performance and achieve delivery goals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed smart pacing approach can effectively improve campaign performance and achieve delivery goals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claims are textually identical and assert the same result with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The delivery pace of each campaign is learned from both offline and online data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The delivery pace of each campaign is learned from both offline and online data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements are equivalent in content and certainty about how the delivery pace is learned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of the proposed approach in a real DSP system is presented. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Implementing the proposed approach in a real DSP system demonstrates its effectiveness in managing campaign performance under delivery constraints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication moves beyond merely presenting an implementation to claiming demonstrated effectiveness under constraints, which changes and strengthens the original contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A prediction scheme is introduced that utilizes a causal pre-selection step to reduce the number of possible predictors to the most predictive set of causal drivers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A prediction scheme is introduced that utilizes a causal preselection step to reduce the number of possible predictors to the most predictive set of causal drivers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim is essentially identical in formulation and intent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal scheme can be less computationally expensive than commonly used sub-optimal schemes like forward selection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal scheme can be less computationally expensive than commonly used suboptimal schemes like forward selection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance/cost comparison without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method suggests a general framework to apply the optimal model-free approach to select variables and improve predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method suggests a general framework to apply the optimal model-free approach to select variables and improve predictions or learn statistical dependencies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the framework is illustrated on a climatological index of El Niño Southern Oscillation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a corresponding claim about illustration on the ENSO index; instead it adds different theoretical/practical claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The information-theoretic optimality of the proposed method is derived and practical selection criteria are discussed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim presents additional theoretical results and discussion that were not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed sparsity-inducing approach effectively recovers missing samples from an incomplete three-dimensional THz data set. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed sparsity-inducing approach effectively recovers missing samples from an incomplete three-dimensional THz data set. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim and asserts the same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The achieved signal-to-noise ratio for reconstruction of T-shape data from only 5% observation was 19 dB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The achieved signal-to-noise ratio for reconstruction of T-shape data from only 5% observation was 19 dB. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracies of obtained thickness and depth measurements for pharmaceutical tablet data after reconstruction from 10% observation were 98.8% and 99.9%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracies of obtained thickness and depth measurements for pharmaceutical tablet data after reconstruction from 10% observation were 98.8% and 99.9%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported accuracy figures match exactly between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results confirm the accuracy of the proposed method through chemical mapping analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results confirm the accuracy of the proposed method through chemical mapping analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same confirmation via chemical mapping analysis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating prior structure information into Tyler's M-estimator improves estimation accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating prior structure information into Tyler's M-estimator improves estimation accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about improved estimation accuracy is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm effectively minimizes the cost function of Tyler's estimator under prior structural constraints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm is guaranteed to converge to a stationary point of the problems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed estimator achieves a smaller estimation error than benchmark estimators at a lower computational cost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed estimator achieves a smaller estimation error than benchmark estimators at a lower computational cost. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: If the constraint set is geodesically convex, the algorithm converges to a global minimum. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves a significant performance boost over previous state of the art approaches on five major scene classification datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves a significant performance boost over previous state-of-the-art approaches on five major scene classification data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance-boost claim is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation in the transformed feature space incorporates the discriminative aspects of the target dataset and encodes features in terms of general object categories present in indoor scenes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation in the transformed feature space incorporates discriminative aspects of the target data set and encodes features in terms of general object categories present in indoor scenes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A new large-scale data set of 1300 object categories commonly present in indoor scenes is introduced. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach exploits rich mid-level convolutional features to categorize indoor scenes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure-preserving property of convolutional neural network activations is not substantially helpful in the presence of large variations in indoor scene layouts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves a relative performance improvement of 3.2%, 3.8%, 7.0%, 11.9% and 2.1% on MIT-67, Scene-15, Sports-8, Graz-02 and NYU datasets respectively.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper introduces a new learnable feature descriptor called 'spatial layout and scale invariant convolutional activations' to address challenges in indoor scene categorization.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology efficiently adapts a trained network model for indoor scene classification using only a limited amount of available training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim directly in content and emphasis on limited training data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel 'Spatially Unstructured' layer is incorporated into the Convolutional Neural Network architecture to enhance robustness against spatial layout deformations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new convolutional neural network architecture is designed that incorporates a novel 'spatially unstructured' layer to enhance robustness against spatial layout deformations.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same architectural contribution but with stronger certainty and framing as a designed architecture, so content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed parameter separable MRF DGS method, dubbed MRF ZOOM, breaks the multiplicative computation complexity into an additive one.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MRF-ZOOM is quasi-parameter-separable, breaking the multiplicative computation complexity into additive one.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MRF ZOOM was hundreds or thousands of times faster than the original brute-force DGS method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluations showed that MRF-ZOOM was hundreds or thousands of times faster than the original MRF parameter quantification method.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the measured speedup claim directly and at the same magnitude.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a high precision quantification, MRF can find the right parameter values for a 64x64 imaging slice in 117 seconds.
alignments.rationale: The specific runtime result (117 seconds for a 64x64 slice) and high-precision quantification detail do not appear in the publication text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatial constraints can be used to further speed up MRF ZOOM.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the use of spatial constraints to further accelerate MRF-ZOOM, so this preprint suggestion is not present in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed decision support system predicts the success of a movie based on its profitability by leveraging historical data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiment results showed that the system outperforms benchmark methods by a large margin in predicting movie profitability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system outperforms benchmark methods by a large margin.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel features proposed in the system made great contributions to the prediction of movie profitability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel features proposed in the study made weighty contributions to the prediction of movie profitability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Key factors of movie profitability were analyzed in the research.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A methodology was devised to distinguish real manuscripts from those generated with SCIGen.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A methodology was devised to distinguish real manuscripts from those generated with SCIGen.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology can discriminate real from fake papers with at least 89% accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accessibility and betweenness were identified as useful features for distinguishing real and fake papers, although their relevance depended on the dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accessibility and betweenness were identified as useful features for distinguishing real and fake papers, although their relevance depended on the dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same feature-level findings and dataset-dependent caveat.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Network features can be used to identify scientific gibberish papers as a proof of principle.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Network features can be used to identify scientific gibberish papers as a proof of principle.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same proof-of-principle conclusion about network features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CN-based approach can be combined with traditional statistical language processing methods to enhance performance in identifying artificially generated papers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CN-based approach can be combined with traditional statistical language processing methods to improve performance in identifying artificially generated papers.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the suggestion to combine approaches to improve performance with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new descriptor possesses flexible blocks that can be adaptively fitted to input images to acquire robustness to deformations of glomeruli.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new descriptor possesses flexible blocks that can be adaptively fitted to input images in order to acquire robustness for the detection of the glomeruli.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel segmentation technique employed generates high quality segmentation outputs and the algorithm is assured to converge to an optimal solution.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the claimed segmentation technique details, convergence guarantees, or explicit high-quality segmentation claim present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed descriptor for glomeruli detection presents promising results, and it is expected to be useful in pathological evaluation.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about promising results and expected usefulness in pathology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ease of perceiving depth in autostereograms is dependent on the choice of the basic pattern used for generating them.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ease of perceiving depth in autostereograms depends on the choice of the basic pattern used for generating them.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The $1/f$ noise pattern (pink noise) provides a significant advantage for fast depth lock-in and fine detail detection in autostereograms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Validating the theoretical model predictions contributes to a better understanding of the visual system's binocular disparity mechanisms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relationship between inconsistency indices and the consistency of decision maker's judgments, as well as the prioritization results, is still unclear.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides examples that necessitate distinguishing between measuring PCM inconsistency, measuring the consistency of decision maker's judgments, and measuring the usefulness of the PCM for estimating the priority vector.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Monte Carlo experiments were conducted to study the performance of various inconsistency indices as indicators of the final priority vector estimation quality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the study allow for a deeper understanding of the information contained in inconsistency indices and aid in selecting an appropriate index for specific situations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis allows for a more profound interpretation of the well-known inconsistency characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim matches the preprint's statement about deeper understanding and interpretive value, so it is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new inconsistency characteristic is developed, leading to the proposal of a PCM acceptance approach supported by classical statistical methodology.

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (active contours with free endpoints) with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived evolution laws for the tangential flow of the endpoints enhance the performance of the contour evolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach includes evolution laws for the tangential flow of the endpoints.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the inclusion of evolution laws; the preprint emphasized performance enhancement. The publication's phrasing clarifies the technical content while omitting the performance claim, so classified as clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of a parametric approach and edge-preserving denoising results in a fast method for image segmentation and restoration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method provides a fast solution for image segmentation and restoration.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim of providing a fast method for segmentation and restoration, matching the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Meta learning can improve the performance of a single base learner by utilizing information from base learners and the learning problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Meta learning can improve the performance of a single base learner by utilizing information from base learners and the learning problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conceptual conclusion about meta learning improving a base learner, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent advancements in f-divergence functional estimation have resulted in simple and rapidly converging estimators for estimating bounds on the Bayes error.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advancements in $f$-divergence functional estimation have resulted in simple and rapidly converging estimators for estimating bounds on the Bayes error.
alignments.rationale: This contribution is carried over verbatim into the publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed estimator applies meta learning to slowly converging plug-in estimators to achieve the parametric convergence rate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed estimator applies meta learning to slowly converging plug-in estimators to achieve the parametric convergence rate.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical comparisons of the estimated bounds were conducted on simulated data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical comparisons of the estimated bounds were conducted on simulated data and on features from sunspot continuum and magnetogram images.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes the sunspot image-patch analysis as part of the empirical comparisons, matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our CNN achieves a classification accuracy of 89%, outperforming the current state-of-the-art by 19%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our CNN achieves a classification accuracy of 89%, outperforming the current state-of-the-art by 19%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact numeric performance claim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our CNN classifies twice the amount of categories compared to the current state-of-the-art on an extended egocentric video dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our CNN classifies twice the amount of categories compared to the current state-of-the-art on an extended egocentric video dataset.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN recognizes whether a video is egocentric or not with 99.2% accuracy, which is an improvement of 24% over the current state-of-the-art.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel visualization of CNN kernels as flow fields to better understand what the network learns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multistart next ascent hill-climbing demonstrates superior performance compared to conventional niching and mating restriction techniques in evolutionary algorithms on multimodal problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multistart next ascent hillclimbing demonstrates superior performance compared to conventional niching and mating restriction techniques in evolutionary algorithms on multimodal problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication sentence is effectively identical in meaning and strength to the preprint claim, with only minor wording variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of structure in the space of local optima in certain problem instances hinders optimization algorithms from effectively exploiting information from one optimum to find another. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lack of structure in the space of local optima in certain problem instances hinders optimization algorithms from effectively exploiting information from one optimum to find another. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A brute-force strategy appears to be the most effective method for discovering all optima when no structure exists in the local optima space. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A brute-force strategy appears to be the most effective method for discovering all optima when no structure exists in the fitness landscape. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides insights into the adequacy of hillclimbers and evolutionary algorithms for multimodal optimization based on the properties of the fitness landscape. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Algorithms similar to local variation (LV) can be devised by applying different statistical models and decisions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Algorithms similar to local variation can be devised by applying different statistical models and decisions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed protocols for frequency estimation in the local model for differential privacy run in time polynomial in the number of users and the logarithm of the universe size. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seemingly purposeful and adaptive behavior can develop without specifying any purpose or goal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seemingly purposeful and adaptive rhythmic behavior is developed without specifying any purpose or goal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the behavior as "rhythmic," which narrows and clarifies the type of adaptive behavior claimed while keeping confidence the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The behavior developed through DEP displays a certain level of sensorimotor intelligence. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the claim about "sensorimotor intelligence;" this specific characterization is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DEP rule arises from the underlying mechanism of spontaneous symmetry breaking due to the tight brain-body-environment coupling. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The surprising results arise from the underlying mechanism of spontaneous symmetry breaking due to the tight brain body environment coupling. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes the results to spontaneous symmetry breaking in the same coupling; wording is slightly different but the scientific claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new synaptic rule is biologically plausible and may have been a catalyst in natural evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This neuronal mechanism may have been a catalyst in natural evolution. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The evolutionary-catalyst aspect is preserved; the publication emphasizes the evolutionary possibility similarly (the plausibility aspect is also present but partly rephrased).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new synaptic rule is biologically plausible and may be an interesting target for neurobiological investigation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit framing of the rule as a target for neurobiological investigation that was not separately stated in the preprint (though related notions appeared); this is an added emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide scientific foundations for athletic performance prediction on an individual level. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide strong evidence that the three parameters per runner are related to physiological and behavioural parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the foundational claim more concrete by linking model parameters to physiological/behavioural factors, clarifying the nature of the provided foundations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method, local matrix completion (LMC), performs best in predicting individual running performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model demonstrates a 30% improvement in prediction error over the state-of-the-art in performance prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies and substantiates the "performs best" claim with a specific measured improvement, clarifying the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many documented phenomena in quantitative sports science can be explained based on our findings in a unified way. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings may explain many documented phenomena in quantitative sports science in a unified way. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this conclusion essentially verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our predictor achieves an average prediction error of 3.6 min on elite Marathon performances and 0.3 seconds on 100 metres performances. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds concrete performance metrics that were not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We conjecture that our findings will be vital in exercise physiology, race planning, the study of aging and training regime design. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces additional conjectured implications/applications that were not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ARGO outperforms all previously available Google-search-based tracking models, including the latest version of Google Flu Trends. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ARGO outperforms all previously available Google-search-based tracking models, including the latest version of Google Flu Trends. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ARGO incorporates the seasonality in influenza epidemics and captures changes in people's online search behavior over time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ARGO incorporates the seasonality in influenza epidemics and captures changes in people's online search behavior over time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological/conceptual contribution is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ARGO is flexible, self-correcting, robust, and scalable, making it a potentially powerful tool for real-time tracking of other social events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ARGO is flexible, self-correcting, robust, and scalable, making it a potentially powerful tool for real-time tracking of other social events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about ARGO's broader utility is unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PISA generates spatially coherent yet detail-preserving, pixel-accurate and fine-grained saliency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PISA generates spatially coherent yet detail-preserving, pixel-accurate, and fine-grained saliency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result about saliency generation is identical in wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PISA aggregates multiple saliency cues in a global context, including complementary color and structure contrast measures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PISA aggregates multiple saliency cues in a global context, improving upon previous methods that relied on homogeneous superpixel based and color only treatment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the novelty by explicitly contrasting PISA with previous approaches, but the underlying contribution is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments suggest that PISA convincingly outperforms other state-of-the-art approaches. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments suggest that PISA convincingly outperforms other state-of-the-art approaches. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is retained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A faster version of PISA improves runtime efficiency while maintaining comparable detection accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A faster version of PISA is developed using a gradient-driven image subsampling strategy, improving runtime efficiency while maintaining comparable detection accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the technical approach (gradient-driven subsampling) used to achieve the faster version, clarifying but not changing the claimed result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work creates a new dataset containing 800 commodity images for evaluating saliency detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work creates a new data set containing 800 commodity images for evaluating saliency detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is present in the publication with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-tiered coarse-to-fine cascade framework improves computer-aided detection (CADe) performance markedly across different medical imaging applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed two-tiered coarse-to-fine cascade framework improves sensitivity in automated computer-aided detection systems.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same main contribution — the two-tiered cascade improves CADe sensitivity/performance; wording differs slightly but the claim content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CADe sensitivities improved from 57% to 70% for sclerotic metastases, from 43% to 77% for lymph nodes, and from 58% to 75% for colonic polyps at 3 false positives per patient.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sensitivities improved from 57% to 70%, 43% to 77%, and 58% to 75% at 3 false positives per patient for sclerotic metastases, lymph nodes, and colonic polyps, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The numeric results and conditions are identical between preprint and publication, only minor reordering of phrasing occurred.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained deep convolutional neural network (ConvNet) classifiers generalize well to different medical imaging CADe applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods demonstrate the ability of ConvNets to generalize well to different medical imaging CADe applications.
alignments.rationale: Both assert that ConvNets generalize well across CADe tasks; publication wording slightly reframes as 'methods demonstrate', but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two randomized algorithms can detect the large entries of an approximately sparse sample covariance matrix using only O(np poly log p) operations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two randomized algorithms can detect the large entries of an approximately sparse sample covariance matrix using only O(np poly log p) operations.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication present the same algorithmic result with identical complexity claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational cost of estimating the sample covariance matrix is typically O(np^{2}) operations, which can be prohibitively slow when n and p are large.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational cost of estimating the sample covariance matrix is typically O(np^{2}) operations, which can be prohibitively slow when n and p are large.
alignments.rationale: Limitation statement is verbatim in both versions; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sufficient conditions on the underlying random variable and the number of samples n are derived for the sample covariance matrix to satisfy approximate sparsity requirements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sufficient conditions on the underlying random variable and the number of samples n are derived for the sample covariance matrix to satisfy approximate sparsity requirements.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical conditions claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper develops the exact linear relationship between the leading eigenvector of the unnormalized modularity matrix and the eigenvectors of the adjacency matrix.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper develops the exact linear relationship between the leading eigenvector of the unnormalized modularity matrix and the eigenvectors of the adjacency matrix.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proposes a method for approximating the leading eigenvector of the modularity matrix and derives the error of the approximation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper proposes a method for approximating the leading eigenvector of the modularity matrix and derives the error of the approximation.
alignments.rationale: The approximation method and error derivation claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a complete proof of the equivalence between normalized adjacency clustering and normalized modularity clustering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a complete proof of the equivalence between normalized adjacency clustering and normalized modularity clustering.
alignments.rationale: The proof/equivalence claim is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments show that normalized adjacency clustering can be as twice efficient as normalized modularity clustering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments show that normalized adjacency clustering can be as twice efficient as normalized modularity clustering.
alignments.rationale: The experimental efficiency result is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rank diversity of words follows a universal lognormal distribution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rank diversity follows a universal lognormal distribution across books published in six European languages since 1800.
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies the data domain (books in six European languages since 1800), which narrows and clarifies the scope of the preprint's universal claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three different word regimes of languages are defined: 'heads', 'bodies', and 'tails'.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three different word regimes of languages are defined: 'heads', 'bodies', and 'tails', based on how word ranks change over time.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The core size of languages is similar across all languages studied.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The core size of languages is similar across all languages studied.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Gaussian random walk model can reproduce the rank variation of words over time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Gaussian random walk model can reproduce the rank variation of words over time.
alignments.rationale: The modeling claim is unchanged in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A neural network with unbounded activation functions still satisfies the universal approximation property.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A neural network with unbounded activation functions still satisfies the universal approximation property.
alignments.rationale: The universal approximation claim is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ridgelet transform is what the network learns after backpropagation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ridgelet transform is what the network learns after backpropagation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained network can be obtained by simply discretizing the ridgelet transform, without backpropagation, subject to a constructive admissibility condition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained network can be obtained by simply discretizing the ridgelet transform, without backpropagation, subject to a constructive admissibility condition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical examples imply that some non-admissible cases result in low-pass filtering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some non-admissible cases result in low-pass filtering.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same empirical observation, though phrasing is slightly tightened (dropping 'numerical examples imply').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An uncertain Multi-objective Multi-item Solid Transportation Problem (MMSTP) based on uncertainty theory is presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An uncertain Multi-objective Multi-item Solid Transportation Problem (MMSTP) based on uncertainty theory is presented.
alignments.rationale: The two texts are verbatim and convey the same contribution without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MMSTP is converted into an equivalent deterministic MMSTP using uncertainty theory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MMSTP is converted into an equivalent deterministic MMSTP using uncertainty theory.
alignments.rationale: The result statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deterministic MMSTP is reduced into single objective programming problems by applying convex combination method and minimizing distance function method.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Both single objective programming problems are solved using Maple 18.02 optimization toolbox.
alignments.rationale: Publication explicitly states "Both" problems are solved, which clarifies the preprint's plural wording but does not change scope or confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A numerical example is provided to illustrate the performance of the models.
alignments.rationale: The illustrative numerical example claim is the same in both documents; only the label (conclusion/result) differs in metadata.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The word vectors of relevant tags for a given image rank ahead of the irrelevant tags along a principal direction in the word vector space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The word vectors of relevant tags for a given image rank ahead of the irrelevant tags along a principal direction in the word vector space.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The tagging model developed runs fast given a test image, in constant time with respect to the training set size.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model gives superior performance for the conventional tagging task on the NUSWIDE dataset and outperforms competitive baselines on annotating images with previously unseen tags.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed unified solution for subspace learning methods using the Rayleigh quotient is extensible for multiple views, supervised learning, and non-linear embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed unified solution for subspace learning methods using the Rayleigh quotient is extensible for multiple views, supervised learning, and nonlinear embeddings.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-view embedding methods demonstrate effectiveness on visual object recognition and cross-modal image retrieval.
alignments.rationale: Effectiveness claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods obtain superior results in both visual object recognition and cross-modal image retrieval compared to related methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods obtain superior results in both visual object recognition and cross-modal image retrieval compared to related methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SDProp is a novel adaptive learning rate algorithm that effectively handles noise by preconditioning based on covariance matrix.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel adaptive learning rate algorithm called SDProp that effectively handles noise by preconditioning based on covariance matrix.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach is more efficient and effective than RMSProp and its variant for various neural networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RMSProp's approximation may be inaccurate due to noise caused by stochastic optimization in first order gradients.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A method that combines machine learning and image systems simulation automates the design of image processing pipelines.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The loop-corrected BP method improves over the naive BP method significantly when applied to the square-lattice Ising model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Loop-corrected BP improves over the naive BP method significantly when applied to the square-lattice Ising model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BP method is not satisfactory in treating complicated loop-induced correlations in finite-dimensional lattice models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Loop-corrected BP can be implemented at the coarse-grained region graph level to further boost its performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed combination strategy, IUTIS, leverages the strengths of various change detection algorithms and compensates for their weaknesses. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed combination strategy, IUTIS, leverages the strengths of various change detection algorithms and compensates for their weaknesses. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results demonstrate that starting from simple algorithms can achieve comparable results to more complex state-of-the-art change detection algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results demonstrate that starting from simple algorithms can achieve comparable results to more complex state-of-the-art change detection algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed strategy maintains affordable computational complexity for real-time applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed strategy maintains affordable computational complexity for real-time applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
